items.title: Judge orders pause on signature validation process for Alberta independence petition
items.summary_auto: A judge has granted a month-long stay preventing Alberta’s chief electoral officer from certifying the results of a petition to force a referendum on a proposal for Alberta to separate from Canada.

items.title: 'It means so much': Highway of Tears' family members unveil Pillar of Hope in Prince George
items.summary_auto: Family members of Indigenous women and girls who have gone missing or been murdered along the Highway of Tears gathered in Prince George, B.C., to witness the unveiling of a monument called the Pillar of Hope.

items.title: Kamala Harris says she 'might' make another presidential run in 2028
items.summary_auto: ​Former U.S. vice-president Kamala Harris, who was the Democratic Party's nominee for the presidency in 2024, said on Friday that she was considering running for ‌president again in 2028.

items.title: Rory McIlroy pulls away with birdie binge and sets Masters record with 6-shot lead
items.summary_auto:
Rory McIlroy delivered a dynamic finish
to the Masters on Friday, looking every bit like someone who has
done this before.

items.title: Sentebale, charity that Prince Harry co-founded, sues him for defamation
items.summary_auto: A charity co-founded by Prince Harry in Africa to honour his late mother, Princess Diana, has sued him for defamation after he stepped down as a patron last year.

items.title: Appeal court upholds stay of THC-impaired driving charge in death of 9-year-old Sask. girl
items.summary_auto: Taylor Kennedy was charged with impaired driving causing death after Baeleigh Maurice, 9, died when Kennedy hit her with her truck on Sept. 9, 2021. The Crown appealed a judge's decision to stay the charge over trial delays.

items.title: Deputy minister found breaching ethics rules says she was following diversity mandate
items.summary_auto: Deputy minister of national defence Christiane Fox says she was trying to bring in outside perspectives when she influenced her former department to hire an acquaintance.

items.title: NDP's Leah Gazan calls MMIWG2SLGBTQQIA+ critics 'bigots'
items.summary_auto: NDP MP Leah Gazan is standing by her use of the initialism MMIWG2SLGBTQQIA+ and denouncing those who are mocking her online for using the term.

items.title: Lockdown lifted at University of Ottawa, man arrested nearby
items.summary_auto: The Ottawa Police Service says it's arrested a man in connection with an incident at the University of Ottawa Friday afternoon that left the school under lockdown.

items.title: Police arrest suspect after Molotov cocktail thrown at home of OpenAI's Sam Altman
items.summary_auto: Officers arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's San Francisco home on Friday and then making threats at the company's headquarters, police and the company said.

items.title: U.S. allows nearly 4,500 South African refugees since October — and only 3 from elsewhere
items.summary_auto: President Donald Trump has made good on a promise to drastically cut the number of refugees into the U.S., with white South Africans being the top priority over those fleeing conflict, poverty and other threats to their safety from different countries.

items.title: First Nations leaders strongly oppose B.C. plan to pause DRIPA as Eby defends move ahead of confidence vote
items.summary_auto: First Nations leaders in B.C. are urging B.C. NDP MLAs to reject Premier David Eby’s plan to  suspend some sections of the province’s Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act (DRIPA), as the government prepares to bring the proposal forward as a confidence vote.

items.title: U.S., Iran delegations head to Islamabad as Tehran demands measures before talks start
items.summary_auto: Iran's negotiating team arrived in Islamabad on Friday for peace talks with the United States, even as Tehran insisted on measures it said needed to be addressed first, throwing last-minute doubt over the meetings scheduled in Pakistan.

items.title: Shelter-in-place lifted after chlorine gas exposure hospitalized 8 at Victoria swimming pool
Has Trump proven himself unfit for the presidency? | About That
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump’s recent social posts, like his threat to destroy Iran's 'whole civilization,’ have alarmed both critics and allies, leaving Democrats questioning whether he’s fit for the presidency – Andrew Chang shows us how they’re going about it. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: Artemis II capsule will be a 'fireball' on re-entry. David Saint-Jacques knows what that’s like
items.summary_auto: After slingshotting around the moon, the Artemis II mission is expected to splash down in the Pacific Ocean tonight. Canadian astronaut David Saint-Jacques knows exactly what hurtling back to Earth feels like.

items.title: Teenager pleads guilty to 1st-degree murder of Pickering senior
items.summary_auto: A teenager who fatally stabbed an 83-year-old retired kindergarten teacher in Pickering, Ont., in 2025 has pleaded guilty to first-degree murder.

items.title: 5 charged in slaying of teen in Hamilton allegedly linked to tow-truck companies dispute
items.summary_auto: A rolling shootout between multiple vehicles led to the murder of a 16-year-old in Hamilton last year, police said Friday, tying the death of York Region resident Faizaan Awan in March 2025 to a “dispute between two tow-truck companies.”

items.title: Canada is coming off 3 consecutive severe fire years. There are concerning signs for 2026
items.summary_auto: The 2026 wildfire season may get off to a relatively quiet start in Canada but lingering drought and a warm summer could tip the scales towards another severe year, experts say.

items.title: Watch the Canada Reads 2026 book trailers
items.summary_auto: Canada Reads 2026 will take place April 13 to 16, and you can get quickly caught up by watching the animated trailers for each of the five books.

items.title: Nurse says she looked at woman '3 to 5 seconds,' staff overwhelmed before sepsis death at Ontario hospital
Vatican, Pentagon deny report of heated meeting with church's U.S. representative
items.summary_auto: The Vatican and the Pentagon have pushed back on a report that suggested a meeting in January between a U.S.-based cardinal and a member of Donald Trump's administration grew heated.

items.title: Canada's economy adds 14,000 jobs in March after February's whopping losses
items.summary_auto: Canada's economy added 14,000 jobs in March, Statistics Canada said on Friday, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 6.7 per cent.

items.title: AI data centre proposed by Kevin O'Leary exempt from Alberta environmental impact assessment
items.summary_auto: Kevin O’Leary's proposed Wonder Valley project in northwestern Alberta will not need to undergo an environmental impact assessment from the province due to existing water and power systems. However, the province says other assessments are required before permits can be issued.

items.title: Albertans who declared banned guns under Ottawa’s buyback still can’t get compensation
items.summary_auto: More than 7,000 banned guns have been declared in Alberta under the federal government’s gun buyback program, but owners in the province are not able to collect compensation because of an ongoing dispute between Alberta and Ottawa over how the program is meant to operate.

items.title: Immunization rates in B.C. falling since COVID-19 pandemic, data shows
items.summary_auto: Childhood immunization rates for most vaccines have been falling since the pandemic, a CBC News analysis of provincial data has found, raising concerns among public health officials about increased vulnerability to infectious disease.

items.title: Hip-hop pioneer Afrika Bambaataa dead at 68
items.summary_auto: Afrika Bambaataa, a man widely considered one of the main pioneers of hip-hop, died in Pennsylvania of prostate cancer on Thursday, according to his lawyer. He was 68.

items.title: Protecting wildfire crews from the dangers of smoke exposure isn't as simple as it sounds
items.summary_auto: Manitoba's government has relied on disposable N95 masks and training to help its firefighters deal with heavy smoke billowing from wildfires, but protecting them from the noxious gases remains a significant challenge.

items.title: The ostrich con: Arguments to save birds from cull in B.C. were based on falsehoods, evidence shows
Messy dressing room costs northern Ontario hockey team a title series — and possible trip to provincial finals
items.summary_auto: The Northern Ontario Hockey Association has ruled the U18 AA Soo Jr. Greyhounds must forfeit their league championship game against the Copper Cliff Reds after leaving a messy dressing room in Blind River. The Jr. Greyhounds are appealing the decision to Hockey Canada.

items.title: McCain daughter says she’s ‘trapped’ in family company
items.summary_auto: An heiress to the multibillion-dollar McCain french fry empire says she is financially “trapped” by the family-owned company and its policies, which she claims prevent her from selling her ownership stake for a fair price.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Ukrainian forces shot down Shahed drones in Middle East during Iran war
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian military personnel shot down Iranian-designed Shahed drones in multiple Middle Eastern countries during the Iran war, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, describing the operations as part of a broader effort to help partners counter the same weapons used by Russia in Ukraine.

items.title: More remote work accommodation requests for medical reasons being refused, federal unions say
items.summary_auto: Two federal public service unions say more and more employees are being denied requests for accommodations to work remotely, particularly for medical reasons, and are accusing the government of being unreasonable.

items.title: Saab dangles sovereign data centre in Montreal to undercut F-35 fighter contract
items.summary_auto: Saab is pitching a Montreal-based, sovereign data hub to secure Canada's fighter jet contract, arguing it would keep mission-critical data out of U.S. hands. The move challenges Lockheed Martin's F-35 model and amplifies Ottawa's growing unease over data control, AI and strategic dependence.

items.title: Immigration Department informing some 30,000 applicants they may be ineligible for refugee hearings
items.summary_auto: Canada's Immigration Department is sending tens of thousands of refugee claimants letters that they may not be eligible for asylum — and is telling some of them that they should leave immediately.

items.title: Is the toilet the 'most important piece of equipment' aboard the Artemis II?
items.summary_auto: When we sent four humans 406,771 kilometres into space for the first time, we also sent a toilet with them. After all, if the plan is to go farther and farther, then it’s critical we test one of the non-negotiable parts of being human.

items.title: What to expect ahead of Artemis II’s splashdown
items.summary_auto: Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, along with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch, are returning to Earth after travelling around the moon. But before they get their feet on solid ground, they have to get their Orion spacecraft safely through Earth's atmosphere.

items.title: Ontario hospital nurse tells inquest she never spoke to Heather Winterstein on day she died, staff overwhelmed
items.summary_auto: A triage nurse who assessed Heather Winterstein hours before she died of sepsis in the St. Catharines, Ont., hospital says she had little time with the 24-year-old, telling a coroner’s inquest the emergency department staff were overwhelmingly busy, in part due to the pandemic. "Nurses are burned out. Nurses are exhausted,” Andrea Demery said several days into the inquiry.

items.title: You, Me & Tuscany proves that we're killing rom-coms
items.summary_auto: You, Me & Tuscany is hardly the worst thing that could happen to you. Though it would still probably be better if it — and this specific flavour of surface level, escapist rom-com in general — didn't exist.
